author: Dr. Lizard
title: What defines the blast count threshold for low-blast MDS-SF3B1 in both classification systems?
answer: Both systems require less than 2% blasts in peripheral blood and less than 5% in bone marrow.
detail: These thresholds ensure the SF3B1 category captures lower-risk disease with favorable prognosis.
Higher blast counts would move the case into different MDS categories despite SF3B1 mutation presence.
The peripheral blood threshold of 2% is more stringent than the bone marrow threshold.
Both thresholds must be met simultaneously for MDS-SF3B1 diagnosis in either system.
releaseNote: The blast count threshold for low-blast MDS-SF3B1 in both classification systems is less than 2% in peripheral blood and less than 5% in bone marrow.
